summary: Hardened the cross-device runbooks for the blocked product QUIC smoke by documenting the receiver-first certificate handoff, canonical sender and receiver artifact layout, issue #1 coordination templates, platform-specific Linux and macOS duties, and qd completion rules for cross-device evidence.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: docs/CROSS_DEVICE_VALIDATION.md documents the exact product QUIC receiver and sender sequence, including artifact paths, certificate exchange, validation boundaries, and failure handling.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: docs/CROSS_DEVICE_VALIDATION.md now has a receiver-first PRODUCT-QUIC-READY handshake, receive/send command shapes, certificate exchange guidance, product QUIC artifact layout, product QUIC result shape, and explicit non-claims.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: docs/LINUX_ORCHESTRATOR.md documents the Linux sender responsibilities for product QUIC handoff, including evidence collection and issue #1 update expectations.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: docs/LINUX_ORCHESTRATOR.md now has the product QUIC sender command, required sender evidence, and PRODUCT-QUIC-SENDER-COMPLETE issue template.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: docs/MACOS_ORCHESTRATOR.md documents the Mac receiver responsibilities for product QUIC handoff without requiring Nix.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: docs/MACOS_ORCHESTRATOR.md now has the native cargo receiver command, required receiver evidence, and PRODUCT-QUIC-READY issue template.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The runbooks include issue #1 coordination templates for Mac receiver-ready, Linux sender-complete, and blocker updates.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: docs/CROSS_DEVICE_VALIDATION.md contains the shared PRODUCT-QUIC-READY template, while the platform runbooks contain receiver-ready and sender-complete templates tied to issue #1 coordination.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Evidence and qd reports are recorded under evidence/m4-product-quic-handoff-runbook and reports/qd/m4-product-quic-handoff-runbook.
No product runtime, decode, render, presentation, or latency claim is made.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: The changed documentation explicitly labels product QUIC as transport and payload validation only, and evidence/m4-product-quic-handoff-runbook/notes.md records the documentation-only boundary.

realWorldValidation.evidence: Documentation and orchestration hygiene only; no product runtime validation is required for this node.
